apigateway.apikey.CfnAbout: The `AWS::ApiGateway::ApiKey` resource creates a unique key that you can distribute to clients who are executing API Gateway `Method` resources that require an API key. To specify which API key clients must use, map the API key with the `RestApi` and `Stage` resources that include the methods that require a key.

apigateway.apikey.CustomerId.Description: An AWS Marketplace customer identifier, when integrating with the AWS SaaS Marketplace.

apigateway.apikey.Enabled.Description: Specifies whether the ApiKey can be used by callers.

apigateway.apikey.GenerateDistinctId.Description: Specifies whether (`true`) or not (`false`) the key identifier is distinct from the created API key value. This parameter is deprecated and should not be used.

apigateway.apikey.Name.Description: A name for the API key. If you don't specify a name, AWS CloudFormation generates a unique physical ID and uses that ID for the API key name. For more information, see [Name Type](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-properties-name.html).  If you specify a name, you cannot perform updates that require replacement of this resource. You can perform updates that require no or some interruption. If you must replace the resource, specify a new name.

apigateway.apikey.StageKeys.Description: DEPRECATED FOR USAGE PLANS - Specifies stages associated with the API key.

apigateway.apikey.Tags.Description: The key-value map of strings. The valid character set is [a-zA-Z+-=._:/]. The tag key can be up to 128 characters and must not start with `aws:`. The tag value can be up to 256 characters.

apigateway.apikey.Value.Description: Specifies a value of the API key.
